cards.entryTitle: Frank Lloyd Wright and the House Built Around the Car
cards.title: Robie House
cards.creator: Frank Lloyd Wright
cards.notice: Robie House shows horizontal lines, connected spaces, and blurred boundaries between interior, exterior, and landscape.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where the Sidewalk Starts · Big ideas: Thresholds / Liminality · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / Visual and Performing Arts
cards.imageInstruction: /imagesearch exact image of Robie House. Use the real artwork/building/place, not a generated substitute. If rights are unclear, use a source link or LINK button rather than embedding.
cards.rightsNote: Use real photo only for exact buildings/places.

cards.title: Temple Street Parking Garage
cards.creator: Paul Rudolph
cards.notice: Paul Rudolph’s New Haven garage treats car circulation and concrete structure as an architectural statement.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where the Sidewalk Starts · Big ideas: Liminality / Thresholds · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / Visual and Performing Arts

cards.title: Lego Parking House
cards.creator: C. F. Møller / LEGO campus context
cards.notice: The Billund parking house shows how a garage facade can communicate function, brand identity, and playfulness.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where the Sidewalk Starts · Big ideas: Liminality / Thresholds · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / Visual and Performing Arts

cards.title: Parc des Célestins / Parking des Célestins
cards.creator: Michel Targe and Jean-Michel Wilmotte
cards.notice: This Lyon car park turns an underground garage into a visual artwork through Daniel Buren’s “Sens dessus dessous” and the periscope view from the square.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where the Sidewalk Starts · Big ideas: Liminality / Thresholds · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / Visual and Performing Arts

cards.title: Michigan Theater parking garage
cards.creator: Rapp & Rapp / reused theater
cards.notice: A former movie palace became a parking garage, making cultural memory and car storage occupy the same space.

cards.title: 9th Avenue Parkade
cards.creator: 5468796 Architecture
cards.notice: Calgary’s parkade is important because it was designed for future conversion, not only current parking.

cards.title: Grand Park, Los Angeles
cards.creator: Rios Clementi Hale Studios
cards.notice: Grand Park appears as a concrete example of reclaiming car-centered land for public life.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where the Sidewalk Starts · Big ideas: Progress / Home & Wandering · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / Geography / Human Geography

cards.entryTitle: The Sidewalk Between Street and Building
cards.title: Sidewalks as urban infrastructure
cards.notice: A lively sidewalk can become a place for walking, food, conversation, waiting, and informal urban culture.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where the Sidewalk Starts · Big ideas: Liminality / Thresholds · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / Geography / Human Geography

cards.entryTitle: Why Moving Sidewalks Never Took Over the World
cards.title: Moving sidewalks
cards.notice: Moving sidewalks were imagined and built long before cartoon futurism made them famous.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where the Sidewalk Starts · Big ideas: Progress / Prediction · Subjects: Computer Science / Technology / Design / Architecture / Urbanism

cards.entryTitle: The Sidewalk Between Street and Building
cards.title: Hostile architecture
cards.notice: A narrow, broken, or missing sidewalk shows that a city can make walking feel secondary or unsafe.
